{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/68157143?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nathanielmiller23/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nathanielmiller23/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nathanielmiller23/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nathanielmiller23/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/nathanielmiller23", "id": 68157143, "login": "nathanielmiller23", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjY4MTU3MTQz", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nathanielmiller23/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nathanielmiller23/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nathanielmiller23/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nathanielmiller23/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nathanielmiller23/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/nathanielmiller23", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-28975", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 6.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-409", "name": "Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification)"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "### Impact

When `NIOHTTPRequestDecompressor` is configured with `.ratio(N)`, the decompression limit is enforced using the `Content-Length` header value from the incoming request rather than the actual number of compressed bytes received. Since `Content-Length` is attacker-controlled, a malicious client can supply an inflated value that causes the ratio check to always pass, effectively disabling the configured decompression limit.

This allows an attacker to send a small, highly-compressed payload (a "gzip bomb") with a falsified `Content-Length` header to bypass the ratio-based protection entirely. The server will decompress the payload without limit, consuming unbounded memory and potentially causing denial of service.

For example, a gzip payload containing highly repetitive data can achieve amplification ratios of several hundred to one. Under `.ratio(10)` such a payload should be rejected, but if the attacker sets `Content-Length` to match the decompressed size, the check evaluates `decompressed > decompressed * 10` which is always false, and the payload is accepted without error.

Across repeated requests, this allows sustained memory amplification far exceeding the configured limits with no error raised.

### Relationship to CVE-2020-9840

GHSA-xhhr-p2r9-jmm7 (CVE-2020-9840) found that the `.size` limit checked compressed rather than decompressed bytes and recommended `.ratio` as a workaround. This advisory identifies a distinct flaw in the `.ratio` limit itself: it uses the attacker-supplied `Content-Length` header as the denominator rather than actual consumed compressed bytes. The two vulnerabilities are in the same decompression limit enforcement code but involve non-overlapping logic errors.

Users who followed the CVE-2020-9840 workaround by switching to `.ratio(N)` are affected by this vulnerability.

### Patches

Fixed in swift-nio-extras 1.34.1. The fix unifies the request and response decompressor implementations so that both accumulate actual compressed bytes received (`compressedLength += part.readableBytes`) rather than relying on any header-supplied value.

### Workarounds

Use `.size(N)` instead of `.ratio(N)` if a fixed upper bound on decompressed output is acceptable for the application. The `.size` limit is not affected by this vulnerability as it does not reference `Content-Length`.

### Credits

NIOExtras is grateful to @nathanielmiller23 for their reporting and assistance with the process.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00042, "percentile": 0.13299}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-6ph5-fww6-vfwv", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-12T15:08:04Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6ph5-fww6-vfwv", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-6ph5-fww6-vfwv"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-28975"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-12T15:08:04Z", "references": ["https://github.com/apple/swift-nio-extras/security/advisories/GHSA-6ph5-fww6-vfwv", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6ph5-fww6-vfwv"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/apple/swift-nio-extras/security-advisories/GHSA-6ph5-fww6-vfwv", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/apple/swift-nio-extras", "summary": "NIOExtras: NIOHTTPRequestDecompressor ratio limit bypass via inflated Content-Length", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-12T15:08:06Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-6ph5-fww6-vfwv", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.34.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "swift", "name": "github.com/apple/swift-nio-extras"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.34.1"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: NIOExtras: NIOHTTPRequestDecompressor ratio limit bypass via inflated Content-Length
